hypothesis: In the MADE benchmark for ternary and quaternary chemical systems with an oracle query budget of 20, a multi-fidelity agent that uses MLIP screening, calibrated epistemic uncertainty estimates, and a Bayesian-optimization acquisition function with occasional high-fidelity (DFT) validation will discover a statistically significant greater number of novel thermodynamically stable compounds than a MatterGen generative model coupled with diversity-based acquisition.
